plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-337 RP10.P07.M06.S09-RP10.P08.M04.S19 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-337 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP10.P07 failure phase (M06 synthetic fixture tail, M07 test/golden cycle, M08 Hermes evidence cycle, M09 Claude review head, M10 closeout head) and opens the RP10.P08 Hermes evidence foundation (M00-M04: Hermes command matrices, evidence field lists, changed-file/command-result/fixture-summary/blocked-claim/permission-summary/audit-summary/no-real-data receipts, Claude dependency markers, human approval markers, PASS/PASS_WITH_FINDINGS/BLOCK semantics, evidence templates, validation command checks, harness boundary notes, regression receipts, next-gate readiness, documentation updates, operator summaries) across ten sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H10), Claude review packet (C10), and CP00-338 handoff, without opening Hermes runtime receipts, command runtime execution, or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-337 (Risk C, 150 units, RP10.P07.M06.S09-RP10.P08.M04.S19) is a descriptor-only pack closing the RP10.P07 failure phase and opening the RP10.P08 Hermes evidence foundation. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claims are fully supported: (1) all 7 required public exports are defined in service.js/validators.js and re-exported via index.js; (2) deliverable/micro-title/phase distributions are internally consistent and match the prompt baseline, the live closeout-pack-plan.json CP00-337 entry (deviation_from_plan: false), the review baseline, and INTAKE_CORE_CP337_REQUIREMENTS; (3) descriptor-only / no-real-data / no-write boundaries hold — freezeCp337Result sets ~40 runtime/DB/storage/Hermes-receipt/lock/rollback/retry/real-data flags to false, the contract no_write_attestation matches, and a targeted grep found NO actual IO (no fs/child_process/fetch/http/process.env/DB operations), only boolean attestation fields; (4) the authority boundary is correct — claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-338, so Claude remains a read-only reviewer and enterprise trust is human-gated; (5) Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic evidence identifiers/receipt-shape strings (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime; (6) CP178+ review hardening is satisfied — claude_packet.invalid_review_blockers covers auth-failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, missing refs, and final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims, and the readiness auth preflight genuinely passed (exit 0, 1432-byte valid JSON, is_error:false). The command-evidence reports production_ready:false (status: implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout); the pack does not over-claim readiness, and production_ready_candidate is correctly scoped to coverage.valid && foundation.valid. The validator performs a strict deepEqual between the contract and the generated descriptor/requirements/attestation and asserts historical_pack_bindings ends with CP00-337; all listed gate commands report exit_code 0. The changed-file scope cleanly separates the 8 active implementation refs from unrelated dirty files (none omitted). No P0/P1/P2/P3 issues found. This PASS affirms descriptor-level consistency and boundary compliance only; it does not grant enterprise trust, which correctly remains subject to human final approval.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-337 intake core p07 closeout p08 hermes foundation descriptor pack
